reasoning:
Elon Musk's net worth as of mid-August 2025 is approximately $352B-$357B, giving him a massive lead of $50B-$100B over the next closest individuals (Larry Ellison and Mark Zuckerberg, who are in the $250B-$303B range). Crucially, a reported July 2025 SpaceX tender offer values the private space company at ~ $400 billion. Since Musk owns approximately 42% of SpaceX, this locks in an incredibly stable chunk of his net worth (nearly $170 billion) that is immune to daily stock market fluctuations. 

While his other major holding, Tesla, remains highly volatile and has trended downward in 2025 (costing him around $80 billion YTD), the stabilization from SpaceX and the recent board approval of his 96 million share interim award provide a strong floor. For Musk to lose the title by December 31, 2025, TSLA would have to suffer another catastrophic drop (around 30-40%) AND Oracle or Meta would have to rally another 15-20% simultaneously over a short 4.5-month span. While possible given tech volatility, the sheer size of the gap and lack of time make it quite unlikely, aligning with the market's ~80% confidence level.
